name: Bank of Commerce
description_short: Bank of Commerce is an FDIC-insured community bank based in Duncan, Oklahoma, offering personal and business deposit accounts, loans, and digital banking services.
description_long: Bank of Commerce is a traditional community bank headquartered in Duncan, Oklahoma, with FDIC insurance backing all deposits. The bank has been serving individuals and businesses with full-service banking capabilities, as evidenced by their comprehensive online presence and range of financial products. As a regulated financial institution, Bank of Commerce operates under federal oversight and maintains a routing number (103112992) and NMLS license (#405284), indicating legitimate banking operations. The bank offers dual service lines for personal and business customers. Personal services include checking, savings, money market, and CD accounts; personal loans, auto loans, and credit cards; online banking, bill pay, mobile banking, and mobile deposit. Business customers can access comparable deposit products plus specialized services like merchant card services, remote deposit capture, ACH cash management, and equipment financing. Both segments benefit from digital tools including CardHub, eStatements, and a switch kit to facilitate account transfers. Bank of Commerce positions itself as a personalized alternative to larger national banks, with the tagline "You're Unique. Your bank should be, too!" They emphasize community involvement through scholarship applications and career opportunities, suggesting local market focus. The website highlights their mobile-first approach and comprehensive online capabilities, allowing customers to "bank from anywhere at anytime." As a traditional regional bank, Bank of Commerce serves customers seeking FDIC-protected deposit accounts and relationship banking from a smaller, community-focused institution. However, the website does not disclose loan terms, interest rates, minimum account balances, or fee structures in detail. Potential customers cannot evaluate specific competitiveness or suitability without contacting the bank directly.
services: Mobile deposit and remote deposit capture
ACH cash management and treasury services
Equipment financing and real estate loans
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full faith and credit of U.S. Government backing
Comprehensive digital services including mobile banking app, online bill pay, and mobile deposit
Dual personal and business service lines with specialized offerings like merchant services and remote deposit capture
Agricultural lending specifically offered, supporting rural and farm-based customers
Multiple account types (checking, savings, money market, CDs) for personalized financial planning
ACH cash management and treasury services for business customers
Community involvement and scholarship programs indicating local commitment
cons: Limited geographic presence as a regional bank; customers outside Duncan, OK service area may face inconvenience
Website does not disclose specific interest rates, APRs, fees, or minimum balance requirements, requiring customers to contact bank directly
No information about loan approval criteria, terms, or competitive rates for personal loans and auto loans
Limited details on credit card rewards, benefits, or terms compared to national card issuers
No indication of whether business credit cards or personal credit cards are issued in-house or through partners
best_for: Oklahoma residents seeking FDIC-protected deposits and relationship banking from a community institution
Small business owners needing personalized business banking with merchant services and ACH management
Agricultural and farm-based operations requiring specialized lending products
Customers prioritizing local bank presence and community involvement over national bank convenience
diagnosis: Bank of Commerce is best suited for individuals and small businesses in the Duncan, Oklahoma area seeking traditional FDIC-insured banking with personalized service and community focus. The main caveat is that the bank does not publicly disclose competitive rates, fees, or loan terms on their website, requiring direct contact to evaluate whether their pricing is competitive with regional or national alternatives.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Bank of Commerce is an FDIC-insured bank in Duncan, Oklahoma, offering personal and business deposit accounts, loans, and digital banking services.
